skill.name: two-sample-mr-research-planner
skill.description: Generates complete two-sample Mendelian randomization (MR) research designs from a user-provided research direction. Use when users want to design, plan, or build a study using two-sample MR to test causal relationships. Triggers:"design a two-sample MR study", "build a publishable MR paper", "test whether this biomarker causally affects this disease", "generate Lite/Standard/Advanced MR plans", "screen multiple exposures with MR", "bidirectional MR design", "causal inference using GWAS summary statistics", or "I want to study X and Y using MR". Always outputs four workload configurations (Lite / Standard / Advanced / Publication+) with a recommended primary plan, step-by-step workflow, figure plan, validation strategy, minimal executable version, and publication upgrade path.
skill.summary: Designs publishable Mendelian randomization studies from a single research question
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 43 static analysis findings across 4 files (548 lines). All 36 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives caused by the scanner confusing Mendelian Randomization (MR) methodology with cryptographic algorithms. All 4 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code formatting, not shell execution. All 5 'system reconnaissance' findings refer to legitimate research methodology prompts and R package function calls. The 3 'hardcoded URL' findings point to legitimate academic GWAS resources and the skill's own GitHub repository. The skill provides legitimate MR research design guidance with no executable code, network exfiltration, or data collection. Risk level set to low because risk factors (network references, markdown code examples) are technically present even though all static findings are false positives.

content.user_title: Design Mendelian Randomization Studies for Causal Research
content.value_statement: Planning a two-sample MR study requires complex decisions about instruments, sensitivity analyses, and GWAS data sources. This skill generates complete study designs at four workload levels with a recommended primary plan, step-by-step workflow, and publication upgrade path.
actual_capabilities: Generate four workload-specific MR study plans (Lite, Standard, Advanced, Publication+)
Provide IV count benchmarks and weak-instrument risk assessment by exposure class
Recommend GWAS data sources with ancestry matching guidance
Include sensitivity analysis plans for heterogeneity, pleiotropy, and leave-one-out
Generate R code frameworks using TwoSampleMR and MRPRESSO packages
Enforce methodological hard rules for valid MR study design
limitations: Does not execute R code or access GWAS databases directly
Cannot verify GWAS data availability or download summary statistics
Does not generate actual statistical results or p-values
Limited to two-sample MR design only; not suitable for RCT or observational study design

use_cases.title: Single Exposure Study Design
use_cases.description: A researcher wants to test whether a specific biomarker causally affects a disease outcome using two-sample MR with a complete sensitivity analysis suite.
use_cases.target_user: Epidemiology researcher with a focused causal question

use_cases.title: Multi-Exposure Screening
use_cases.description: A researcher needs to screen multiple dietary or lifestyle factors against a single disease outcome with appropriate multiple testing correction.

use_cases.title: Bidirectional MR Planning
use_cases.description: A researcher suspects reciprocal causation between two traits and needs a design that tests both directions with instrument strength checks.
use_cases.target_user: Molecular epidemiologist studying complex trait relationships

prompt_templates.title: Quick Lite Study Design
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to study the causal effect of [exposure] on [outcome]. Please design a Lite two-sample MR study plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a fast preliminary MR assessment before committing to a full study

prompt_templates.title: Standard Publication-Ready Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a two-sample MR study to test whether [exposure] causally affects [outcome]. Include sensitivity analyses and validation plans.
prompt_templates.scenario: User aims for a standard peer-reviewed publication

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Exposure Screening Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen [list of exposures] for causal associations with [outcome] using two-sample MR with FDR and Bonferroni correction.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to screen multiple exposures against one outcome

prompt_templates.title: Full Publication+ Design with Bidirectional MR
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a comprehensive two-sample MR study for [exposure] and [outcome] targeting a high-impact journal. Include bidirectional testing, MVMR, and colocalization.
prompt_templates.scenario: User targets a high-impact journal publication

output_examples.input: I want to investigate the causal effect of serum uric acid levels on gout risk.
output_examples.output: Generates four workload configurations: Lite (2-4 weeks, one exposure by one outcome), Standard (4-8 weeks with full sensitivity suite), Advanced (8-14 weeks with bidirectional testing), and Publication+ (12-20 weeks with colocalization and power analysis). Primary recommendation: Standard configuration with IVW as primary estimator and MR-Egger, weighted median, and weighted mode as complementary estimators.

output_examples.input: I need to screen 12 dietary factors for causal associations with colorectal cancer.
output_examples.output: Provides per-factor IV count estimates of 2 to 15 IVs per dietary trait with high weak-instrument risk flag. Recommends FDR correction for 5 or more exposures and Bonferroni correction for 10 or more exposures. Prioritizes exposures with strongest biological prior and suggests weighted median estimator for traits with fewer than 10 instruments.

best_practices: Specify the exposure class clearly so the skill applies the correct IV benchmarks and risk assessment
Use the Standard or Advanced configuration for publication-quality designs with sensitivity analysis
Review GWAS ancestry matching guidance before selecting data sources to avoid ancestry mismatch bias
anti_patterns: Requesting a full design without specifying the exposure and outcome leads to a generic output
Skipping the Lite configuration for a feasibility check before a full study can waste resources
Ignoring weak-instrument flags and proceeding with IVW when instruments are weak produces unreliable results

faq.question: What is two-sample Mendelian randomization?
faq.answer: Two-sample MR uses genetic variants from separate GWAS datasets as instruments to test causal effects between an exposure and an outcome.

faq.question: What outputs does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates study plans with four workload configurations, recommended GWAS sources, sensitivity analysis plans, and R code frameworks.

faq.question: Does this skill run R code?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates R code frameworks as documentation examples. You must run the code yourself in your R environment.

faq.question: Which GWAS databases are recommended?
faq.answer: The skill references IEU Open GWAS, UK Biobank, FinnGen, and other public resources. See the references file for details.

faq.question: Can this skill verify whether my GWAS data is available?
faq.answer: No. You must check data availability yourself using the IEU Open GWAS API or the relevant consortium website.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for non-MR study designs?
faq.answer: No. This skill covers two-sample MR only. Use other skills for RCTs, observational studies, or systematic reviews.
